articles.headline: 'Finally': Norway star Erling Haaland on fulfilling World Cup dream
articles.description: Erling Haaland tells ESPN he is ready to make his World Cup debut and show his love for Norway on football's biggest stage.

images.name: Erling Haaland [1296x729]
images.caption: Erling Haaland has 55 goals in 50 games for Norway.
images.credit: Jared C. Tilton - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: World Cup legends and celebrities reveal their tournament predictions
images.caption: World Cup legends and celebrities reveal their tournament predictions

articles.headline: Jordan Henderson: Luka Modric is the best midfielder I've faced
articles.description: Jordan Henderson has praised Luka Modric as the greatest midfielder he has ever faced, ahead of England's match against Croatia on Wednesday.

images.name: Jordan Henderson Luka Modric [1296x729]
images.alt: Jordan Henderson challenges Luka Modric in the 2018 World Cup semifinal.

images.name: Henderson hails Modric as 'best' midfielder ahead of WC opener
images.caption: Henderson hails Modric as 'best' midfielder ahead of WC opener

articles.headline: World Cup Daily Live: New Zealand's match-predicting puppy poops on live TV
articles.description: Enjoy all the action from Monday's World Cup matches, with features, news, video analysis and color from around the competition.

images.name: Oli Sail, Tova O'Brien and Chris Chang with Bear the dog on TVNZ's Breakfast [1080x608]
Lone Curaçao supporter in Berlin celebrates goal vs. Germany
images.caption: Lone Curaçao supporter in Berlin celebrates goal vs. Germany

articles.headline: World Cup Last Dance: Courtois, Belgium's golden goalkeeper, gets one more go
articles.description: Belgium's 'Golden Generation' were the world's No. 1 team, but didn't win a trophy to show for it. Now, Thibaut Courtois is one of the last ones left.

images.name: Belgium's Thibaut Courtois World Cup 2026 portrait [1296x729]
images.credit: Steph Chambers - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Tielemans: Belgium coming to the World Cup to win
images.caption: Youri Tielemans is targeting World Cup glory as his Belgium side travel to their Seattle base camp.

articles.headline: Rod Stewart pictured at Scotland World Cup win shortly after cancelling concert due to illness
articles.description: Sir Rod Stewart was seen celebrating Scotland's World Cup win over Haiti at Gillette Stadium in Boston on Saturday night shortly after cancelling a concert due to illness.

images.name: Rod Stewart [1296x729]
images.alt: Rod Stewart celebrates at the game between Scotland and Haiti.
images.credit: (Photo by Martin Rickett/PA Images via Getty Images)

articles.headline: Explained: How a chip in the ball allowed World Cup's fastest-ever goal by a sub to stand
articles.description: VAR overturned an offside decision after ruling there was a slight flick on the ball by Sweden's Alexander Isak, thanks to some technology in the ball.

article.headline: Colombia, Ivory Coast 0-0 at halftime in World Cup
article.description: Colombia, Ivory Coast 0-0 at halftime in World Cup
article.linkText: Colombia, Ivory Coast 0-0 at halftime in World Cup

images.name: Yaya Touré (Ivory Coast) Shot Blocked at 69'
images.caption: Yaya Touré (Ivory Coast) Shot Blocked at 69'

article.story: BRASILIA, Brazil -- Starved of the World Cup action for 16 years, Colombia -- and its fanatical traveling supporters -- are certainly making the most of being back on football's biggest stage. Driven on by a partisan pro-Colombian crowd that turned the Estadio Nacional into a sea of yellow, the South Americans scored through James Rodriguez and substitute Juan Quintero in a devastating six-minute spell in the second half to beat Ivory Coast 2-1 on Thursday. A second victory in Group C, following a 3-0 win over Greece, helped Colombia qualify for a place in the last 16. A 0-0 draw between Japan and Greece in the later group match ensured Colombia would progress to the next stage. "We have had two wins with players who are playing their first World Cup," Colombia coach Jose Pekerman said. "We're happy to have defeated such a good opponent. We have a lot of young players, and this is going to help them grow." Known for his midfield artistry, playmaker Rodriguez showed his prowess in the air by getting clear of Ivory Coast captain Didier Drogba -- again only used as a substitute -- at a corner to head in Colombia's opener in the 64th minute, lighting up a disappointing match. The celebrations had hardly died down among the vast hordes of Colombia supporters by the time a defensive mistake by Ivory Coast midfielder Serey Die allowed Teofilo Gutierrez to play in Quintero, and the midfielder slotted home a low finish. Ivory Coast replied with a brilliant 73rd-minute solo goal by Gervinho, his second of the World Cup, but couldn't find a second despite a strong finish. The Ivorians remain on three points after their 2-1 opening win over Japan. Instead of Radamel Falcao, the country's star striker who was ruled out through injury before the tournament, the most important factor in Colombia's march through the group stage has arguably been its amazing support. With supporters wearing yellow shirts dominating the stadium, it was effectively an away game for the Ivorians. "It was extraordinary," Rodriguez said, "it was very much in our favor that Colombia could win." The crowd roared with relief at the final whistle, with Drogba's introduction in the 60th minute helping pep up Ivory Coast's attack that had previously looked rudderless with Wilfried Bony as the lone striker. "I must say that we didn't deserve to lose," Ivory Coast coach Sabri Lamouchi said. "It was very experienced Colombia team who waited for us to make mistakes." Drogba may now start that match but Lamouchi will also be looking for more from the squads top player, Yaya Toure, who seems like a shadow of the player who starred for Manchester City in the English Premier League last season. There was no mistaking the passion of the Ivory Coast lineup -- Die was in tears during the national anthem -- but the team's undoubted attacking quality has been lacking so far at this World Cup and Colombia looked more threatening going forward. The best chance of a cagey first half was spurned by Gutierrez, who miscued a volley with the goal at his mercy after being perfectly set up by Rodriguez's left-wing cross in the 28th. Otherwise, the highlight of the first 45 minutes came when a large inflatable ball bounced onto the playing surface, causing a brief stoppage. Tricky winger Juan Cuadrado struck the bar with a fierce, angled shot before teammate Rodriguez, named man of the match, broke the deadlock with the kind of header that Drogba -- the player supposed to be marking the Monaco playmaker -- has been used to delivering. Quintero's side-footed finish gave Colombia breathing space, meaning that Gervinho's goal -- he jinked through two defenders, beat another and shot inside goalkeeper David Ospina's near post -- proved to be just a consolation. Colombia faces Japan in its last group game.

keyEvents.text: First Half ends, Colombia 0, Côte d'Ivoire 0.
Substitution, Colombia. Juan Fernando Quintero replaces Víctor Ibarbo because of an injury.
Didier Zokora (Côte d'Ivoire) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Côte d'Ivoire. Didier Drogba replaces Wilfried Bony.
Goal!  Colombia 1, Côte d'Ivoire 0. James Rodríguez (Colombia) header from the centre of the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Juan Cuadrado with a cross   following a corner.
Substitution, Côte d'Ivoire. Salomon Kalou replaces Max Gradel.

Goal!  Colombia 2, Côte d'Ivoire 0. Juan Fernando Quintero (Colombia) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Teófilo Gutiérrez with a through ball   following a fast break.
Substitution, Colombia. Santiago Arias replaces Pablo Armero.
Substitution, Côte d'Ivoire. Mathis Bolly replaces Serey Dié.
Goal!  Colombia 2, Côte d'Ivoire 1. Gervinho (Côte d'Ivoire) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Arthur Boka.
Substitution, Colombia. Alexander Mejía replaces Abel Aguilar.
Cheick Tioté (Côte d'Ivoire) is shown the yellow card.
